title: carex saxatilis var. inferalpina
intro: Carex saxatilis var. infernalpina, also known as the alpine sedge, is a variant of the Carex saxatilis plant species, native to the high-altitude regions of the Alps. The alpine sedge has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the alpine sedge is typically harvested in the summer months and dried for use in teas, infusions, and tinctures.
